insights.title: Late-Night Blue Light Exposure Delays REM Onset
insights.summary: Screen interaction extending past 11:30 PM consistently correlates with a 40-minute delay in deep sleep cycles.
insights.evidence: On June 5th and June 7th, high screen time (340m and 410m) directly preceded drops in next-day focus scores to 62 and 45.
insights.missing_data_explanation: None. Device hardware telemetry was 100% complete during these intervals.

insights.title: Sub-optimal Context Switching Spike
insights.summary: Rapid bouncing between applications indicates attention fragmentation during peak working hours.
insights.evidence: Micro-habits show continuous 30-second app alternation cycles between 2:00 PM and 4:30 PM on June 8th.
insights.missing_data_explanation: Low confidence score (68%) due to missing browser extension tracking data between 3:00 PM and 3:45 PM on Monday.

timeline.description: Established healthy routine. Average focus remained steady at 82 with normalized physical rest indicators.
Gradual increase in screen-boundary extensions. Minor deep sleep disruptions began logging over midnight cycles.
Sustained high cognitive loads coupled with early waking routines caused a visible crash in daily productivity metrics.
Active attempt at boundary regulation. Weekend recovery is apparent, but weekday work windows show persistent volatile fatigue spikes.
